name: Coze Studio
tagline: All-in-one visual platform for building, debugging, and deploying AI agents
seoTitle: Coze Studio: Open-Source Visual AI Agent Platform
metaDescription: Coze Studio is an open-source, all-in-one platform for building AI agents and apps. Use visual no-code and low-code tools for prompts, RAG, plugins, and workflows.
overview: Coze Studio is an all-in-one AI agent development tool that brings together the core pieces you need to ship an agent: prompts, RAG (knowledge bases), plugins, and workflows. It is the open-sourced core engine of the Coze Development Platform, which has served tens of thousands of enterprises and millions of developers.

The visual design and build tools let you create, debug, and deploy agents, apps, and workflows using no-code or low-code approaches. Its backend is written in Go, the frontend uses React and TypeScript, and the overall system follows a microservices architecture built on domain-driven design (DDD) principles for performance and easy customization.
features: Visual no-code and low-code canvas to build, debug, and publish AI agents, apps, and workflows
Model service that manages a model list and integrates providers such as OpenAI and Volcengine
Build resources including plugins, knowledge bases (RAG), databases, and prompts
Drag-and-drop workflow builder to structure business logic and task processing
OpenAPI for creating conversations and initiating chats, plus a Chat SDK to embed agents in your own app
Microservices backend in Go with a React and TypeScript frontend, built on domain-driven design

gettingStarted.intro: Coze Studio is self-hosted via Docker. You need a machine with at least 2 cores and 4 GB of RAM, with Docker and Docker Compose installed and the Docker service running.

steps.heading: Clone the repository
steps.body: Retrieve the source code from GitHub.

steps.heading: Deploy and start the service
steps.body: Move into the project folder and start the service. The first run may take a while to pull and build images. On macOS or Linux use the make target; on Windows copy the example env file and run Docker Compose directly. When you see "Container coze-server Started," the service is up.
steps.code:
cd coze-studio
# for macOS or Linux
make web
# for Windows
cp ./docker/.env.example ./docker/.env
docker compose -f ./docker/docker-compose.yml up

steps.heading: Register and configure a model
steps.body: Visit http://localhost:8888/sign to register an account with a username and password. Then add a model at http://localhost:8888/admin/#model-management (the image version must be 0.5.0 or higher). Finally, open Coze Studio at http://localhost:8888/ to start building.

useCases: Building and publishing AI agents that use knowledge bases, plugins, and workflows to handle real business tasks
Creating low-code AI apps and visual workflows that turn a few dragged nodes into running business logic
Embedding a built agent or app into your own product through the OpenAPI and Chat SDK, for example a web-based customer service assistant
